Generating Registration Tokens for Cloud LM Licensing

To configure your application to work with a Cloud License Manager (LM) license, you need a Registration Token. This token is used to create an Authentication Token, which allows you to claim sessions on the license. You must log in to the Sentinel EMS customer portal using an email address set as administrator by Uniface license management. Administrators can generate these tokens themselves on the Sentinel EMS customer portal, as long as they have this feature enabled for their licenses. The license owner has complete control over the Registration Tokens.

Note: If you are not an administrator on the Sentinel EMS Portal, you must ask your system administrator for a Registration Token.
Registration Tokens have the following attributes:
  • Count—each token can have a count up to one million.
  • Expiry—a token is valid for up to a maximum of 30 days. This can be extended in the Sentinel EMS customer portal for up to 30 days each time.

To generate a Registration Token:

  1. Log in to the Sentinel EMS Customer portal.
  2. If you have not already done so, activate your Entitlement by selecting your Entitlement ID (EID) on the Entitlements screen and clicking Activate.
  3. On the navigation pane on the left, select Access Management > Add Registration Tokens
  4. On the Registration Tokens page, click Add Registration Token.
  5. On the Add Registration Token page, select the Expiration Date to set the Registration Token's validity. This can be a maximum of 30 days. The token must be registered within this validity period.
    Note: You can manually extend the expiration date at any time. This can be done multiple times. Once a Registration Token has been used to generate an Auth Token, that Auth Token can be reused by the same machine or container, even if the Registration Token has expired.
  6. In the Identifier field, enter a value with letters and numbers to create an ID for the particular token. You can leave this blank, and an ID will be generated automatically.
  7. In the Count field, specify the number of times this Registration Token can be used to generate Auth Tokens. The Auth Token is automatically generated in the background when the Registration Token is configured using ulic /reg= or the installer.
    Note: You can manually increase the count at any time while the Registration Token is still active, up until a maximum count of one million. However, the number of concurrent sessions allowed is determined by your Entitlement, and not the Registration Token count.
  8. Click Save.

The generated Registration Token can now be given to users to register. The token is only needed the first time it is registered, and must be used within the expiration period set.

Note: Once an Authentication Token has been generated, it remains active, unless manually deleted on the Sentinel EMS customer portal, or in the case of a Docker container, the container is destroyed.

To edit the Registration Token, for example to increase its count, click the pencil icon in the Actions column.

To manage cloud LM sessions, see Managing Cloud LM Sessions and Tokens.

Related Links
  • Troubleshooting Cloud LM Licensing Issues

    The following section provides resolution for the error which occurs while registering your Registration Token.

  • Configuring Cloud LM Licenses

    To configure your Uniface application to connect to a Cloud LM Entitlement, you need to enter your Registration Token during installation or run the Uniface License Management utility (ulic) after installing Uniface. If you are using Cloud LM to license your Uniface Docker image, you can enter the registration token REGTOKEN environment variable when mounting your image.
